mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC below -0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate output that outpaces rate hike headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative or flat 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 4.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue bases, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate signals unsustainable consumer leverage, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production increases during a post-shock recovery phase confirm supply-side normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery regimes yield negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.
